Now you can maintain tolerance groups for the posting of physical inventory differences to define the maximum value difference a user is allowed to post in physical inventory. For this configuration step, you define tolerance groups and assign user to the tolerance groups.

Figure 2: Configuration Step 'Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences''

Watch the video below in which I show you the configuration step and its impact on the physical inventory count.

Video 4: Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Special Stock E & K,  restricted-use stocks in SAP FIORi apps for performing stock changes 

You can use different stock types and special stock types to perform stock changes. The following table gives you an overview about the stock types and special stock types that are supported by the SAP Fiori apps Manage Stock, Transfer Stock - In-Plant, and Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant. Blue highlighted stock types are newly supported since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. In detail: 

  • The special stock types Order Stock (E) and Consignment Stock (K) have been added to the Manage Stock and the Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant apps.
  • You can now also display the stock type Restricted-Use Stock in the Transfer Stock - In-Plant and Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant apps.

Figure 3: Supported Stock Types and Special Stock Types in SAP Fiori apps for Goods Movement

In the next video I show you the use of the new stock types and special stock types in the SAP Fiori apps for performing stock changes.

Video 5: Use of the stock types and special stock types that are newly supported in the SAP Fiori apps 'Manage Stock', 'Transfer Stock - In-Plant', and 'Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant'

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

Warehousing Management

Check out the blog post Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Release – What’s New in which Christian Ries shares the innovations for Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release.

In this blog post I highlight 3 innovations from Warehouse Management that my colleague Markus Oertelt demonstrated in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series, and share the demo videos with you.

Warehouse-Driven Split of the Outbound Delivery Order

Now you can split packed and unpacked items, quantities, or handling units (HUs) from an existing outbound delivery order into a new outbound delivery order.
Use this feature to remove parts of an outbound delivery order that, for example, are not ready to be shipped or for which there is no space on the transport, by splitting those parts into a new outbound delivery order.
You can split the outbound delivery order either before or after warehouse processing has started with created or confirmed warehouse tasks. Access this feature through the Warehouse Monitor. Two new functions are available in the Warehouse Monitor for the following nodes:

  • Outbound Delivery Order --> Outbound Delivery Order Item --> More Methods --> Split Open Qty into new ODO
  • Documents --> Handling Unit --> More methods --> Split into new ODO

Watch the next video in which Markus Oertelt shows the warehouse-driven split of the outbound delivery order through two different use cases in the system:

  1. Use case - Complete picking is not possible
  2. Use case - Lack of space in the truck

Video 6: Warehouse-Driven Split of the Outbound Delivery Order

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Generation of Packaging Items

For packed outbound deliveries packing material items can be generated automatically. The packing materials are posted goods issue in Warehouse Management (WM) and in Inventory Management (MM-IM). Generated items can be billing relevant. Items are generated with goods issue or with ready for shipping.

In the next video Markus Oertelt shows the new feature in the system and how to set it up in configuration. 

Video 7: Generation of Packaging Items

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Partial Goods Receipt Posting

Now, you can post goods receipt for one or several handling units (HUs) that belong to the same delivery. You can post goods receipt for parts of the inbound delivery that are packed into HUs (Partial Goods Receipt). 
You can access this feature through the Warehouse Monitor. Two new functions are available on the level of the Handling Unit node:

  • Goods Receipt
    This function posts goods receipt for one or more HUs.
  • Reverse Goods Receipt
    This function reverses goods receipt for one or more HUs.

Watch the next video in which Markus Oertelt shows the new feature in the system.

Video 8: Partial Goods Receipt Posting

This feature is also available when you use radio frequency (RF) devices to carry out warehouse activities.

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

Transportation Management

Exciting product updates coming your way for Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. I want to encourage you to read the latest blog post from Markus Oertelt 

  • Transportation Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 

that includes an amazing system demo about Supplier Return with Transportation Management and many more interesting topics.

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

More Information / Additional links

You will find a collection of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product centric blog posts from Markus Oertelt and me here:

  •  SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Manufacturing & Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management – The Link Collection
  • Discrete Industries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - The Link Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and @GerhardWelker.

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a xx-minute-long recording and a presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below: 

 

️Thanks for reading this blog post. Stay tuned!

(🔙 Table of Contents)

entry.title: Leveraging Key User Extensibility in BRIM - Transfer data from Billable Items to FI documents
entry.content:
Have you ever wondered how to transfer an information from your incoming billable item to an FI document in your BRIM solution?

In my blog article, I’ll will show you how to extend your BRIM solution using the Custom Fields app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public and Private Edition)!

Content:

  • The sample requirement and solution approach
  • Creation and configuration of the sample custom field
  • Conclusion
  • Appendix

The sample requirement and solution approach

In a BRIM implementation with Convergent Invoicing (CI) and Contract Accounting (FI-CA) it is a common requirement to transfer a custom information from the billable item (BIT) to the item of an FI document.

The Key User Extensibility app Custom Fields allows you to extend your BRIM solution with additional, custom fields and to copy the information from a source to a target object. When you create a custom field using the app, you choose a Business Context, which determines which object is extended. The configuration of your custom fields allows you to enable Business Scenarios, a business scenario controls the automatic transfer of data. The value in the custom field is always copied from source to target, even if the source field is empty.

The diagram below provides an overview of the active Business Contexts and Business Scenarios you need to forward an information from the billable item to the FI document.

Overview active Business Contexts and Business Scenarios

Creation and configuration of the sample custom field

The following section provides basic information on how to make a custom information from your billable item object available in the subsequent Convergent Invoicing and Contract Accounting objects and finally in your ACDOCA document in FI.

1. Launch the Custom Field app (F1481) and create a new field. Choose business context Convergent Invoicing: Billable Items. In our example, the field is labelled MTBitText, it’s of type Text with a length of 20 characters. The identifier is automatically proposed by the app.

Initial field creation

2. The detailed configuration of a custom field allows you to enable several features. In our example, we want to include the new field in the relevant Fiori apps, add it to the API for billable item creation and forward it to the billing document item (Business scenario: Billable Items to Billing Document Item).

Once you have completed the configuration, publish the new field.

The screenshots below show the sample configuration:

Enabled user interfaces

Business Scenario

SOAP API

3. Once the publishing process is complete, you will notice that your field has also been automatically created for the business context Convergent Invoicing: Billing Document Item. Don’t worry, this is the intended feature, not a bug! Since we activated the business scenario Billable Items to Billing Document Item, the app generates the custom field for the target business context as well.

Now proceed with configuring your field across the remaining objects. Start with the context Convergent Invoicing: Billing Document Item and finish with Accounting: Journal Entry Item. Use the diagram from the first chapter as a guide to identify the necessary business contexts and scenarios.

Remember to activate not only the business scenarios, but also the user interfaces. Depending on your specific requirements, you may also need to enable your custom field for OData services and Form Templates.

Remark and warning:

  • You can include additional fields to the total records transferred from FI-CA to the general ledger. To do this, you must activate the Contract Accounting Business Partner Item to Journal Entry Item or Contract Accounting General Ledger Item to Journal Entry Item business scenarios.
  • Think twice if you really need to have the information in FI! If you update totals records with additional fields, the number of totals records and journal entry items may increase considerably.

After completing all the configurations, make sure to publish your custom field for all relevant business contexts.

Business contexts

4. Now it’s time to test the configuration.

4a. Create test billable items that include a value in your custom field.
The new custom field is available on the billable item and the value is shown.

Manage Billable Items

4b. Execute the billing and invoicing process in Convergent Invoicing.

The value of your custom field is copied by the business scenario from source to target.

Manage Billing Documents

Manage Invoicing Documents

4c. Transfer the reconciliation key from Contract Accounting to FI.

As usual, you can customize the grid layouts and include your field in them.

Maintain Reconciliation Key (FPF3)

Manage Journal Entries

Conclusion

From my perspective, the key user extensibility apps offer a powerful yet user-friendly solution for custom extensions in your daily work as a BRIM consultant. Additionally, they help keep the core of your BRIM solution clean, ensuring that each change remains fully compatible with future releases.

Now it’s up to you: Explore the capabilities and features of Key User Extensibility in BRIM!

I hope you find this blog post useful and that you can benefit from this feature in your projects!

 

Appendix

Apps:

  • F1481 - Custom Fields

Documentation:

  • Documentation for F1481-Custom Fields app
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud – BRIM – Extend your system
  • SAP S/4HANA – Convergent Invoicing Extensibility (OP)
  • How to transfer existing fields in a custom include to Custom Fields app

 SAP Convergent Invoicing SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management SAP Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable

entry.title: Safe (and happy) Cycling!
entry.content:
We’ve all become experts at constant movement, whether we’re joining new groups, coaching our friends, or planning our next big adventure.

SAP innovations are delivered in cycles that are familiar to you, putting solutions in your hands that simplify everyday work, increase your effectiveness, and secure your success. Because we know that your finance and HR teams collaborate as they plan and reconcile budgets with employee position and financing requirements, we are releasing continuous improvements to our platform that give you easier, real-time controls. And yes, we've built in AI-powered insights, recommendations, and automation. 

SAP’s public sector solution uses SAP SuccessFactors HCM alongside SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Business AI, for end-to-end management of strategic, financial, HR and operational decisions for budgeting and forecasting. In the upcoming release cycles (2H2024 and 1H2025) you’ll be able to manage position budget control with all the compliance and automation that you’ve come to expect from your industry-minded neighbors here at SAP. See how people are managed, and if positions are funded, alongside grant reservation and commitments. Look forward to conversations with colleagues that shape policy and efficiently run programs, and make informed, fast financial and operational decisions with live access to intuitive dashboards and simulation tools. Speed along your chosen path to people assignments, finish payroll and accounting runs, and complete the financial reporting of budgets and grants; proactively monitor employee costs, use automated anomaly detection and prevention to mitigate fraud risks and reduce losses. Adopt these features at your own pace.

Subscribe to keep moving ahead: SAP SuccessFactors What's New and SAP S/4HANA Cloud 

It’s a comprehensive and unified approach that increases any organizations’ stewardship of taxpayer money. No matter if your agency has 500 people, 5,000 people, or is larger: we help governments and not-for-profits make data driven decisions that carry their mission forward.

entry.title: Two-tier ERP and Group Reporting: Deployment Options with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Potential Two-tier ERP Customers running their operational ERP systems on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition or SAP S/4HANA on-premise can connect to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition to perform group reporting in different ways subjected to business requirements. Below three are most used type of deployments 

  1. Connect SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition subsidiaries with Group Reporting Data Collection (GRDC)
  2. Run Group Reporting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition connecting to private cloud ERPs
  3. Connect SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition subsidiaries with Central Finance

 

1.Connect SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition subsidiaries with GRDC -  Leverage Group Reporting & integration with the set up at Headquarters running on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition when subsidiaries are running on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition

Key capabilities with this type of deployment includes

  • Subsidiaries can run on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition
  • Financial statements & trail balances can be uploaded to GRDC at Headquarters.
  • Mostly Group Financial Consolidation is performed at Headquarters running on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition.

     

Note : 

 *     GRDC can be deployed both on S/4HANA Public Cloud or Private Cloud Edition

 Data upload/replication can be manual or via SAP delivered Integration

 

2. Run Group Reporting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition connecting to private cloud ERPs - Group Profitability can be measured in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition which is typically performing Group Reporting on behalf of Headquarters when subsidiary is running on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition

Key capabilities with this type of deployment includes

  • Running Group Reporting in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition that leverages latest Group Reporting innovations delivered through continuous upgrades
  • Financial statements & trail balances can be uploaded to GRDC 
  • Any ERPs that cannot (yet) transition to the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition can stay in private cloud

     

Note : 

 *     GRDC can be deployed both on S/4HANA Public Cloud or Private Cloud Edition

 Data upload/replication can be manual or via SAP delivered Integration

 

3.Connect SAP S/4HANA public cloud edition subsidiaries with Central Finance - Group Consolidation and profitability can be measured at Headquarters running on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition with Central Finance

Key capabilities with this type of deployment includes

  • Real-time accounting document replication to central finance system running on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 
  • Subsidiaries can run on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition

     

Note: Customers can further evaluate each deployment option to select the one that best suits their business requirements and organizational needs.

entry.title: Integrated Product Cost Planning across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
With Integrated Financial Planning, SAP delivers content in SAP Analytics Cloud for financial planning and budgeting. This content comes with prepared integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Analytics Cloud where in the following a closer look is taken at the cost estimate integration from SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition to SAP Analytics Cloud.

Basis of this integration is the cost estimate calculation in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition which is used in Integrated Financial Planning process “Product Cost Planning” to define standard costs for finished goods. These standards costs are then used in Integrated Financial Planning in the profitability and P&L planning.

Cost Estimate in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition Product Cost Planning

In Product Cost Planning as part of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, cost estimates are used to estimate the cost of produced goods and services. The cost estimate calculation for a given product requires to maintain the manufacturing related components and quantities (bill of material in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) as well as the work centers and work center activity consumptions process (routing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition). Cost estimate calculations are heavily used in the manufacturing industry.

For Integrated Financial Planning, the previously calculated cost estimates from Product Cost Planning in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition are read and a complex and resource-intensive explosion process takes place at this step. This is an iterative process that goes through different cost estimates to determine for products via semi-finished products the raw materials and the quantities as well as the activities and activity consumption. Furthermore, it skips cost estimate calculations for raw materials, the identified cost estimates are split according to a cost of goods manufactured typical component breakdown like raw materials, activities, etc. and further applied settings that are all defined as filters. Finally, it is ensured that according to the filter settings for each product the most current cost estimate is considered.               

New API Service to optimize the resource-intensive cost estimate reading process

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408, SAP optimized this resource-intensive integration as part of a new API service by introducing buffer tables when reading cost estimates from Product Cost Planning in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

These buffer tables are optimized for the integration with SAP Analytics Cloud and where the results from the cost estimate reading process are stored (after the previously mentioned algorithm and transformation). This also means that the new API service is no longer directly based on the Product Cost Planning in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but rather accesses the provided itemization in the previously explained buffer.

Therefore, the new integration includes two new apps to fill the buffer with cost estimate results. More precisely, with the first app cost-estimate-related filter settings along with configuration IDs are defined for the cost estimate reading and transformation process and then used in the second app to run preparation jobs where the actual process is executed, and the buffer is filled accordingly.

Using the new API service at the begin of this article, for the integration the buffered results (also referred to as itemization) are then read into SAP Analytics Cloud using the previously defined configuration ID.

The new API service still serves primarily to support product cost planning in Integrated Financial Planning, but due to its flexibility, other planning use cases in SAP Analytics Cloud are now also possible.

Further solution details, the comparison of the old and new API service and the interaction with the product cost planning in SAP Analytics Cloud is depicted in the following figure:

More information about this optimized integration is available in the following online help: Importing Cost Estimate to SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Capacity Levelling using Capacity Scheduling Table in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Capacity Levelling using Capacity Scheduling Table 

This blog highlights the Realignment functionality in the app ‘Capacity Scheduling Table’ in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This app caters the possibility to schedule orders finitely, thereby helping the planner to level the capacity to have a smooth production schedule avoiding capacity overload. Here we will provide some fundamental clarification on the following topics. 

  1. Usage of Set Strategy 
  2. Possibility of finite planning 
    • Realignment
    • Rescheduling 

Usage of Set Strategy

In capacity scheduling table, Set Strategy is used to pre-set up the strategies that would be used during rescheduling of orders. The set strategy provides the following options:

Figure 1: Set Strategy ( App: 'Capacity Scheduling Table')

Note:

  • The strategy is applicable to Reschedule Action and other planning actions e.g. dispatching. For Realignment, the additional realignment pop-up overwrites the set strategy.
  • The planning mode ‘Bucket’, scheduling control ‘Find Slot’ and direction ‘Backward’ remains as default unless changed. 

Planning Mode:

This is used to show the planning mode in the detail object page of the orders

You can select any one of the following modes:

  • Bucket- Displays capacity duration in buckets (sum of all shifts excluding breaks). You can select day, week or shift as bucket size.

Figure 2: Detail View in Time Bucket( App: 'Capacity Scheduling Table')

 

  • Sequence- represents the daily view of the capacity duration in a time continuous way.

Figure 3: Detail View in Sequence ( App: 'Capacity Scheduling Table')

 

Scheduling Control

The following options help to control the scheduling strategy (finite or infinite) of the order operations :

  • Find Slot (Finite): Schedule an operation in a slot that has sufficient capacity, considering the existing capacity of the work center.
  • Infinite Scheduling: Schedule an operation in a slot without considering the current capacity load.
  • Insert Operation (Finite): you can insert an operation into an existing schedule on a desired date even though there is no available slot on this date, or the slot is too small. If required, the app creates a slot that is large enough to hold this order by shifting the neighboring operation that are in the planning direction onto the work center or resource.
  • Note (for using insert operation)
  • The scheduling direction is considered when you choose this option
  • You cannot insert an operation in the middle of an existing operation.
  • An ongoing operation cannot be modified. 

 

 

Possibility of Finite planning for Capacity Levelling:

Realign Schedules:

This functionality helps primarily to:

  • “Resolve overdue orders” in case production is slower than planned
  • “Pull forward” for orders in case production is faster than planned
  • Resolve capacity overload

Overdue orders: are termed as overdue when the operation end date has passed the current time without the order being dispatched or confirmed. Overdue status doesn't depend on Planning status (dispatched or non-dispatched). The overdue alert state is calculated based on the current date and the scheduling direction you have set in Set Strategy. The overdue alert icon will not be displayed for partially confirmed orders.

Late orders are termed late if the receipt date of the order is greater than its requirement date.

You can identify the overdue and late orders by the alert icons and accordingly prioritize the dispatch of those orders or move your scheduled orders to an earlier time.

Realignment Schedules could be used for smoothing the production schedule for the portfolio of orders using filter on a pacemaker work center by capacity levelling.

 During realignment user can choose either one of the scheduling controls to realign your work center schedules. This control overrides the control of set strategy.

  • Find Slot
  • Insert Operation

After selecting the control, select an offset time period (Future or Past) and enter the number of calendar days the chosen strategy should apply for. To use past you need to set a past scheduling threshold in "My App Settings".

Note

  • You cannot opt for Find Slot if your offset value is in the past.
  • The Realign button applies to your changes and the Save and Realign button saves your settings for the next visit and applies to your changes.
  • Realignment schedule also dispatches the order at the same time.
  • Realign Schedule works only forward regardless of the scheduling direction you have chosen.

 We are using the following apps to provide a pictorial understanding on the topic in relation to the app ‘Capacity Scheduling Table’:

  • Manage Work Center Capacity
  • Capacity Scheduling Board

 The following illustration from app ‘Manage Work Center Capacity’ shows the overload of the work center due to overdue orders before realignment: 

Figure 4: Detail View of Workcenter ( App: ‘Manage Workcenter Capacity’)

 

Now we perform the following steps in the app ‘Capacity Scheduling Table’ 

  • Filter your orders using the Alert Status and Work Center filter. The Orders table displays all overdue or late orders as per your selection.
  • Select the check boxes against the overdue or late order operations you want to resolve. You can select all orders of a particular time bucket as well. 

Figure 5: List View ( App: 'Capacity Scheduling Table')

  • Choose Realign Schedules.
  • Choose any one of the scheduling controls: OPTION 1 - using ‘Find Slot’
  • Choose a time Offset.
  • Enter number of calendar days
  • Choose – Realign and Save 

Figure 6: Realign Pop-up ( App: 'Capacity Scheduling Table') 

  • User can choose any one of the following actions:
    • Realign: To apply your changes to the system, so that the planner can review the planning simulation before saving it.
    • Save and Realign: To save the current settings for your next visit and to apply your changes
    • Cancel: To discard your changes

After saving the realignment the result could be checked in the app ‘Manage Work Center Capacity’. 

Figure 7: Detail View of Workcenter ( App: ‘Manage Workcenter Capacity’) 

After realignment using finite planning, the load is distributed based on available capacity of the work center without creating any overload, thus levelling the capacity and smoothing the production schedule. The same could be checked in the app ‘Capacity Scheduling Board’ as well where no overlap would be noticed. 

Figure 8: App: ‘Capacity Scheduling Board’

 For realignment, we can use OPTION 2 – ‘Insert Operation’ as well. 

Load before realignment (we reverted the scenario) 

Figure 9: Detail View of Workcenter ( App: ‘Manage Workcenter Capacity’)

 You can follow the steps 1-8 mentioned above,

In step 4 (from the previous steps), choose the other scheduling control.

OPTION 2 - using ‘Insert Operation’ 

Figure 10: List View ( App: 'Capacity Scheduling Table')

 

Figure 11: Realign pop-up ( App: 'Capacity Scheduling Table') 

It can be noticed that after operation realignment, the load was shifted to a date in the future for the selected order operation.

Figure 12: List View ( App: 'Capacity Scheduling Table') 

The load on 28.08.24 has been decreased and one of the order operations was inserted on 17.09.24 and thus increase of load is visible. 

Figure 13: Detail View of Workcenter ( App: ‘Manage Workcenter Capacity’)

If you want to smoothen the capacity for the entire time bucket, realignment using find slot could be done. e.g. realignment using find slot from 28.08.24 

Figure 14: Detail View of Workcenter ( App: ‘Manage Workcenter Capacity’)

Same can be checked in the scheduling board as well. 

Note:

  • When the pacemaker work center is used in multiple operations, the load might not reduce completely because, realignment takes place for the first operation only. That means you might still see overload (decreased) on that work center since the other operations are not realigned.
  • If all operations shown in the work center object page in the app ‘Manage Work Center Capacity’, are the same shown in Capacity Scheduling Table, then overload caused due to overdue order will be resolved by realignment. (Evaluation and realignment for same number of operations for the same pacemaker work center in both apps). 

 

Reschedule

  • Reschedule: Enables the user to change the date and time of the order operation that has been dispatched. You can set the firming status for deallocated planned orders. You can reschedule any order irrespective of its planning status (dispatched or non-dispatched).

Note

If the Scheduling Control is forward, the start date of the order operation can be modified. (In case of scheduling direction “forward”: Specify new start date + time). 

If the Scheduling Control is backward, the end date of the order operation can be modified. (In case of scheduling direction “backward”: Specify new end date + time). 

Figure 15: Detail View of Workcenter ( App: ‘Manage Workcenter Capacity’)

The color legend could be adapted by providing the threshold in the app settings. 

Figure 16: List View ( App: 'Capacity Scheduling Table')

In this case we are using backward rescheduling. 

Figure 17: Reschedule Pop-up ( App: 'Capacity Scheduling Table')

Figure 5: Reschedule Message ( App: 'Capacity Scheduling Table') 

Because of the set strategy the overload was mitigated during the rescheduling of the overload operation.

Figure 18: Set Strategy ( App: 'Capacity Scheduling Table') 

The rescheduling has scheduled the overloaded operations according to the finite available slots.

Figure 19: Detail View of Workcenter ( App: ‘Manage Workcenter Capacity’) 

The color legend could be adapted by providing the threshold in the app settings. Here orange color represents the optimum load between 80-100%.

Happy Reading!

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our new community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Landscape of the Future
entry.content:
Every once in a while, I force myself to write something not too technical. Last time I did this was for the blog “SAP HANA & Data Warehousing for non-experts” from May 2019:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-hana-data-warehousing-for-non-experts/ba-p/13413212 .

Reading this blog 5 years later, it surprises me how much has changed. For starters, the following software products have become “deprecated” or “sunset”. “Sunset” is SAP vocabulary for “dead” or “almost dead”:

  • “Native HANA Data Warehousing” aka “Calculation Views”;
  • the marketing term “Leonardo” for “Intelligent Technologies” (but not the intelligent technologies themselves);
  • an on-premise data orchestration tool named “Data Hub”, later renamed to/ replaced by “Data Intelligence”, which also has a Software-as-a-Service or SaaS-version;
  • a SaaS-version data warehouse named “Data Warehouse Cloud” was not even mentioned by me in the blog. Good for me, because it turned out to be not long-lived.

But no need to mourn about these products. SAP has taken large steps toward the goals they then had, e.g. delivering options to create virtual data flows as opposed to data flows with data storage or “data persistency”. Or in other words the goal to “leave the data where it is”. This is mainly due to a new software product named “SAP Datasphere”. Datasphere is a SaaS offering, it is the successor of Data Warehouse Cloud, thus a data warehouse, and it contains functions from the data orchestration tool Data Intelligence. The expectation is that in time Datasphere will contain more functions from Data Intelligence up to a level that Data Intelligence can be “sunset” … deprecated … buried.

The future of all system landscapes is clear: systems need to be Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) aka Public Cloud. The necessary components of an analytics landscape are also clear: source systems, a data warehouse and a frontend tool. With the birth of SAP Datasphere, it became possible to build such a landscape with modern SaaS-version components from SAP. See picture below of this “SAP Analytics Landscape of the Future”.

Basis is the trio of systems SAP S/4HANA, SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) connected to one another in a triangular fashion. So let’s start with these three.

SaaS-version SAP S/4HANA, or “SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition” as it is officially called, is the future-proof choice for ERP data, i.e. transaction data for the financial and logistics processes that every company shares with other companies. Compared with on-premise or private cloud version S/4HANA, Saas-version S/4HANA is much more difficult to customize or extend to support specific business requirements. Two types of extensibility remain: “in-app extensibility”, available via tiles like “Custom Fields & Logic”, “Custom CDS Views” and “Custom Analytical Queries”, and “side-by-side extensibility”, which comes down to taking the data out of S/4HANA and carrying out all the custom-specific stuff in a separate system like SAP’s Business Technology Platform (BTP). SaaS-version S/4HANA provides two flavors of “embedded analytics”. One is “analytical queries” created via in-app extensibility tiles “Custom CDS Views” and “Custom Analytical Queries”. For more detail on this type of embedded analytics, I recommend this book written by someone I know quite well:
https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-1-4842-7017-2
The second flavor is “Embedded SAC”. More about this when I talk about SAC.

New kid-on-the-block SAP Datasphere is a combination of cloud data warehousing and data orchestration. What it brings into the play in addition to S/4HANA and SAC is data integration and data persistency. Some data integration options are available in SaaS-version S/4HANA, but that is mainly limited to connecting master data to transaction data. SAC also provides data integration functions, e.g. the “Shared Dimension” function, but combining two transaction data sources requires a union-operation, which is not available in either S/4HANA or SAC. Datasphere on the other hand provides the complete toolbox for data modelers: joins, unions, aggregations, filters, the works. And it continuously gives the option to choose between virtual and persistent data processing. Talking about choice options: Datasphere also continuously gives the option toc choose between developing via direct coding or via a graphical shell. Data persistency is the second function Datasphere brings into the play. Some data persistency functionality is available in SAC, but SAC is by no means a data warehouse. To give my concise review of the Datasphere product: it is good stuff!

System number three is full-use SAP Analytics Cloud. It provides four main functions:

  1. Visualizations with real-time data via a Live Connection
  2. Visualizations with imported data via an Import Connection
  3. Planning (financial, supply chain, HR, …)
  4. Smart Forecasting = machine learning in planning

Especially the planning capabilities of full-use SAC are quite strong. SAC was originally developed as a planning product. The reporting capabilities came later. SAC is the recommended planning solution within the SAP portfolio, and in that sense the formal successor of SAP BPC. What we now see happening at customers is that SAC is chosen for planning, and consecutively also used for visualizations. Full-use SAC receives strong competition from Microsoft PowerBI, but only for function #2, visualizations with imported data.

As mentioned earlier, SaaS-version S/4HANA includes an “Embedded SAC” system. From the four functions of SAC listed above, Embedded SAC only delivers function #1: visualizations with real-time data. No planning or data persistency functions, unfortunately. But, hey, visualizations are cool and it’s free!

Let’s have a closer look at how the three components of the SAP Analytics Landscape of the Future – S/4HANA, Datasphere and SAC - are connected. The arrow from S/4HANA to Datasphere is of course the ETL (Extraction, Transformation & Loading) connection between a source system and its data warehouse. All kinds of standard content is available to pick up data from the S/4HANA system and transfer it to the DataSphere system, either virtually or persistently. Especially useful are functions to selectively pick up only data that has not been transferred yet, a function known as “delta-mode extraction”. Another clear arrow goes from the DataSphere system to SAP Analytics Cloud, the connection between a data warehouse and its frontend tool. Two types of connection are available: “Import” = persistent data transfer, and “Live” = virtual data access. I am always careful if SAP used the term “seamless integration” – it often implies hard labor in some sort of machine language 😉 – but for the integration between Datasphere and SAC the term is more than justified. In the upper right corner of the screen, one can toggle (!) between the two systems. Also, look-and-feel of the two systems is almost identical, giving the impression that one is working in one system instead of two. Well done, SAP! But why are there also arrows going from S/4HANA to SAC and back? This has to do with the planning functions of SAC, which were there long before Datasphere existed. The planning process is extraction of actuals data from S/4HANA to SAC, creating planning versions in SAC, and “retraction” of the final version plan data from SAC to S/4HANA.

One of the main functions of Datasphere is cross-system data-integration. This is the main application area where S/4HANA embedded analytics falls short, and a data warehouse like Datasphere is needed. With regard to cross-system integrations, first systems springing to mind is SAP’s still growing group of cloud systems other than S/4HANA. A far from complete list is SuccessFactors for HR, Ariba for procurement, Concur for travel, Fieldglass for time writing, etc. etc.. Like S/4HANA, these systems can be relevant for planning in SAC, e.g. SuccessFactors for workforce planning. A more complete list of SAP cloud systems can be found here:
https://learning.sap-press.com/sap-and-the-cloud
One can expect standard content being and becoming available for connections between these SAP cloud system and Datasphere. Of course, one can also connect non-SAP systems, either cloud or on-premise, to Datasphere, but here the support with standard content will be much less.

Let’s go crazy, and close this blog with some closing remarks:

  • With S/4HANA, SAP continues to produce the best ERP software in the world, and with the Public Cloud Edition it delivers an excellent cloud offering.
  • With SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP successfully moved the business planning functionality to the cloud combined with visualization functionality.
  • And finally, Datasphere is SAP’s first serious SaaS offering for data warehousing, thus completing SAP’s analytics landscape of the future.

Feedback is much appreciated. Especially, did I exceed in writing something not too technical? Please let me know.

entry.title: Importing Scope from the Digital Discovery Assessment into SAP Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
Are you leveraging the digital discovery assessment toolkit for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition? If so, you'll be pleased to know that you can seamlessly import your scope selection from there into SAP Central Business Configuration with just a few clicks. This integration between the two tools has been available for several releases now, providing greater convenience and efficiency for users.

Screenshot of the Select Country/Region dialog featuring the Import Scope from JSON File button

Once you’ve completed your scope selection in the digital discovery assessment, the next step is to export it as a JSON file. This file can then be easily imported into SAP Central Business Configuration when defining your scope. The JSON file must be maintained in a specific format, which is automatically generated when exporting from the digital discovery assessment. For use cases going beyond that, a sample JSON file can be downloaded from SAP Central Business Configuration. This file can be manually adjusted as needed and be used as a template.

Screenshot of the Import Scope from JSON File dialog including the button to download a sample file

Upon import, a dialog will notify you of the success of the import and whether any imported scope requires your attention. It's important to note that the import process does not impact any previous scope selections made within SAP Central Business Configuration.

Screenshot of the Warning dialog pointing to imported scenarios that need special attention

To learn more, you can go to this SAP Help Portal page for step-by-step instructions and further details about this topic. And to stay in the know, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag. Or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have an idea on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the SAP Customer Influence portal to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: Where can I learn to to streamline and automate bank statement processing?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering Bank Statement Processing and Account Clearing in SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey empowers business users and consultants to streamline and automate bank statement processing. In doing so, they will be able to boost efficiency in the finance sector and effectively utilize the capabilities of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Learning Objectives

  • Describe cash collection and reconciliation​
  • Define how cash is applied in the system​
  • Describe the Multi-Bank Connectivity option
  • Monitor the status of bank statements
  • Describe configured posting rules​
  • Describe automated posting rules​
  • Reprocess bank statements​
  • Receivables line-item matching​
  • Payables line-item matching​

 

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: User experience and app dev sessions at SAP TechEd 2024 virtual
entry.content:
For 2024, SAP TechEd is virtual once again, with lots of great sessions grouped into six content tracks. If you prefer in-person learning, SAP TechEd on Tour consists of companion events around the world in collaboration with regional user groups (ASUG, SAUG, UKISUG, and DSAG). In this post, I’ll highlight the sessions on user experience and application development, since that’s my main focus as part of the SAP UX Engineering team.

In my many conversations with customers this year, the questions that I hear most are:

  • What is the best / fastest / cheapest path to SAP Fiori?
  • How do I move to SAP S/4HANA with all my existing customizations? I understand the concept of “clean core”, but how do I achieve it?
  • To what extent can I customize my public cloud system?
  • How much AI can I use with my current SAP setup?
  • How are you making it easier to build applications?

Here are the SAP TechEd sessions that cover these topics. Since they are all presented live from our studio in the Heidelberg AppHaus, some might not be during regular business hours in your region. Please check the session catalog to see when they are running in your time zone. If it’s at an inconvenient time, you can watch the recording.

What is the best / fastest / cheapest path to SAP Fiori?

DT104 | Enabling new, AI-driven interactions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Fiori

Enabled by SAP Fiori elements, SAP S/4HANA Cloud includes enhanced built-in AI for improved productivity, quick insights, and collaboration. See the latest user interface of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and how you can take full advantage of SAP Fiori elements through a personalized “My Home” experience, insight cards created by the Joule copilot, collaboration through Microsoft Teams and SAP Collaboration Manager, natural language for filtering SAP Fiori apps list reports, and AI-driven summaries.

Katja Zoch, VP UX Engineering, SAP

Sebastian Steinhauer, VP UX Engineering, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

How do I move to SAP S/4HANA with all my existing customizations? I understand the concept of “clean core”, but how do I achieve it?

DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future

Explore the future of cloud ERP, focusing on the latest innovations and strategies driving digital transformation. Delve into the technological backbone of SAP's vision for cloud ERP, covering in-app extensibility, integration with SAP Business Technology Platform, AI-powered automation, and the "suite-first" approach. Learn the benefits of a clean core and its role in enabling composability, empowering businesses to adapt and evolve, and get insights into this year’s cloud ERP track sessions.

Jan Gilg, President Cloud ERP, SAP

Carine Tchoutouo Djomo, Product Manager, SAP

Track Overview (45 min)

DT101 | Choosing the right path for your business to SAP S/4HANA Cloud

SAP offers different options and guidance to companies transitioning their businesses to cloud that vary from a standardized and controlled option through SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, a flexible option based on their specifications through SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or a combined option through two-tier scenarios. Find out about the important factors, explore the right option for your organization, and get insights into SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408.

Khaled MUSILHY, VP, Cloud ERP Product Management, Head of SaaS Foundation, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

DT200 | Clean core extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Learn how to manage your custom, on-stack extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with the ABAP Cloud development model following clean core principles. Find out about the new guidelines for clean core ABAP development in a well-known, three-tier extensibility model and how to handle transformation options for existing custom code to ABAP Cloud. Get an outlook of upcoming generative AI support and hear our plans for ABAP test cockpit to support governance of your clean core developments.

Olga Dolinskaja, Product Manager, SAP

Thomas Fiedler, Chief Product Expert, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

AD180 | Build extensions with ABAP Cloud and Joule's ABAP developer capabilities

Learn how to develop transactional SAP Fiori apps in ABAP Cloud using ABAP RESTful application programming model supported by generative AI. Explore features such as business events for loosely coupled integration scenarios and read-only "treeviews" for displaying hierarchical data. ABAP Cloud is our comprehensive development model for building clean-core-compliant apps, services, and extensions on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP S/4HANA, both in the cloud and on premise.

Carine Tchoutouo Djomo, Product Manager, SAP

Jump-Start (25 min)

To what extent can I customize my public cloud system?

DT201 | Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Learn about key user and development tools in the ABAP Cloud development model and how customers and partners can manage their on-stack extensions to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition. Learn about the extensibility options, discover insights into integration of SAP Build solutions, and get an outlook on AI support for key users and developers.

Thomas Schneider, Chief Development Architect, SAP

Alda Dollani, Development Architect, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

DT202 | Understand how composable architecture can unleash your business value

The composable enterprise architecture can revolutionize the way businesses of the future operate. In a world where agility, efficiency, and innovation are paramount, the composable enterprise offers the flexibility to pivot and respond to business needs in real time. Learn how our innovative approach of the composable enterprise in the GROW with SAP and RISE with SAP solutions helps unlock greater business value.

Stefan Batzdorf, CTO Cloud ERP, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

AD102 | Unlock SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility with SAP Build

Explore the latest capabilities in SAP Build solutions to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while keeping your core clean. The extensibility wizard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides direct access to various extension options both on stack and side by side on SAP Business Technology Platform. New remote function call connectivity in SAP Build enables clean core extension of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition using tier-two BAPIs.

Florian Buech, Product Management, SAP Build, SAP

Karthik Thirunavukkarasan, Principal Product Manager, BTP ADAI, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

How much AI can I use with my current SAP setup?

DT104 | Enabling new, AI-driven interactions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Fiori

Enabled by SAP Fiori elements, SAP S/4HANA Cloud includes enhanced built-in AI for improved productivity, quick insights, and collaboration. See the latest user interface of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and how you can take full advantage of SAP Fiori elements through a personalized “My Home” experience, insight cards created by the Joule copilot, collaboration through Microsoft Teams and SAP Collaboration Manager, natural language for filtering SAP Fiori apps list reports, and AI-driven summaries.

Katja Zoch, VP UX Engineering, SAP

Sebastian Steinhauer, VP UX Engineering, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

AD104 | SAP Build Code: AI development and new service integrations with CAP

Examine new capabilities of SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) in SAP Build Code, and experience how the Joule copilot can speed up your extension development. In addition, find out how service integrations with CAP can reduce development effort, help you avoid pitfalls, and allow for evolution without disruption following the clean core strategy.

David Kunz, Software Architect for CAP @ SAP, SAP

Daniel Schlachter, Software Architect, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

How are you making it easier to build applications?

AD101 | Tailor your UI with SAPUI5 in SAP Build Code

Discover the latest on SAPUI5 development in SAP Build Code, providing a modern user experience across ABAP and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. Learn how to realize clean core extensibility with new capabilities and how you can future-proof your app with new tools and best practices. Understand why SAP Web Components are strategic and how you can benefit from using them.

Oliver Graeff, Director, Product Management SAP UI5, SAP

Peter Muessig, Chief Architect, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

AD105 | Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build

Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions.

Anne Keller, Chief Development Expert, SAP

Tim Back, Chief Development Architect, SAP BTP, SAP

Lecture (25 min)
